name: geoparser

metadata.name: geoparser
metadata.summary: A customizable geoparsing library for unstructured text

documentation:
# Irchel Geoparser

The **Irchel Geoparser** (hereafter referred to simply as **Geoparser**) is a Python library designed as a complete end-to-end geoparsing pipeline. It integrates advanced natural language processing techniques to recognize and resolve place names (toponyms) in unstructured text, linking them to their corresponding geographical locations. ## Overview Geoparsing involves two main tasks: - **Toponym Recognition**: Identifying place names in text. - **Toponym Resolution**: Disambiguating these names to their correct geographical locations. Geoparser addresses both tasks by combining state-of-the-art language models and efficient algorithms, enabling it to process large volumes of text with high accuracy and speed. ## How It Works 1. **Input Processing**: Users input texts as strings, which are preprocessed using a [spaCy](https://spacy.io/) NLP pipeline. This includes tokenization and named entity recognition to identify toponyms in the form of names of geopolitical entities, locations, and facilities. 2. **Candidate Generation**: For each toponym, the gazetteer database is queried to generate lists of potential candidate locations. This is done using a token-based greedy matching strategy designed to achieve high recall while keeping candidate lists concise. 3. **Textual Representation**: Toponyms are represented using their surrounding context, which is extracted and truncated to meet model input length requirements. Candidate locations are also transformed into text by constructing descriptive sentences using attributes sourced from the gazetteer. 4. **Embedding Generation**: A fine-tuned [SentenceTransformer](https://www.sbert.net/) model is used to encode the textual representations of both the toponyms and their corresponding candidates into embeddings, mapping them into a shared vector space. 5. **Similarity Comparison**: Embeddings of toponyms and their corresponding candidates are compared using cosine similarity. The candidates with the highest similarity scores are then selected as the most likely locations referenced by the toponyms. ## Getting Started To begin using Geoparser, refer to the [installation](https://docs.geoparser.app/en/latest/installation.html) and [usage](https://docs.geoparser.app/en/latest/usage.html) sections of the [documentation](https://docs.geoparser.app/en/latest/). ## Contributing Geoparser is an open-source project, and contributions are welcome. If you encounter any issues or have suggestions for improvements, please open an issue or submit a pull request on the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/dguzh/geoparser). ## Acknowledgments Geoparser originated as part of my Master's thesis and was further developed with support from the [Department of Geography at the University of Zurich](https://www.geo.uzh.ch/). I thank my supervisor, Prof. Dr. Ross Purves, for his insightful feedback, encouragement, and the opportunity to continue this work as part of a research project. ## License Geoparser is released under the [MIT License](https://github.com/dguzh/geoparser/blob/main/LICENSE). It also uses several third-party libraries, each with its own license. For a complete list of these licenses, see the [full license details](https://github.com/dguzh/geoparser/blob/main/THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES) in the repository.

classes.name: geoparser.geospan.geospan.GeoSpan

methods.name: geoparser.geospan.geospan.GeoSpan.__eq__
documentation:
Check equality of GeoSpan with another GeoSpan.

Args:
    other (GeoSpan): Another GeoSpan object to compare with.

Returns:
    bool: True if both GeoSpans are equal, False otherwise.

methods.name: geoparser.geospan.geospan.GeoSpan.location
documentation:
Get the location information associated with this toponym.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Any]: Dictionary containing location information.

methods.name: geoparser.geospan.geospan.GeoSpan.score
documentation:
Get the similarity score for the resolved location.

Returns:
    float: Similarity score between toponym and resolved location.

methods.name: geoparser.geospan.geospan.GeoSpan.context
documentation:
Get the contextual Span around the toponym, truncated to model input size.

Returns:
    Span: The context Span for the toponym.

methods.name: geoparser.geospan.geospan.GeoSpan.get_candidates
documentation:
Get the list of candidate location IDs for this toponym, with optional filtering.

Args:
    filter (Optional[Dict[str, List[str]]], optional): Filter to restrict candidate selection.

Returns:
    List[str]: List of candidate location IDs.
Custom spaCy Span class extended for geoparsing.

classes.name: geoparser.gazetteers.geonames.GeoNames

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.geonames.GeoNames.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the GeoNames gazetteer.

Inherits from LocalDBGazetteer and sets the gazetteer name to 'geonames'.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.geonames.GeoNames._create_location_description
documentation:
Create a textual description for a location using GeoNames data.

Args:
    location (Dict[str, Any]): Dictionary containing location attributes.

Returns:
    str: Textual description of the location.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.geonames.GeoNames._read_file
documentation:
Read a GeoNames data file and yield data in chunks.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the data file.
    columns (Optional[List[str]], optional): List of column names.
    skiprows (Optional[Union[int, List[int], Callable]], optional): Rows to skip.
    chunksize (int, optional): Number of rows per chunk.

Returns:
    Tuple[Iterator[pd.DataFrame], int]: Iterator over DataFrame chunks and total number of chunks.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.geonames.GeoNames._populate_locations_table
documentation:
Populate the 'locations' table with data from GeoNames datasets.

Executes an SQL query to insert processed data into the 'locations' table.
Gazetteer implementation for GeoNames data.

variables.name: geoparser.constants.MAX_ERROR
documentation: half Earth's circumference in km

classes.name: geoparser.geodoc.geodoc.GeoDoc

methods.name: geoparser.geodoc.geodoc.GeoDoc.__init__
documentation:
Initialize GeoDoc with geoparser and standard Doc arguments.

Args:
    geoparser (Geoparser): The Geoparser instance associated with this document.
    *args: Variable length argument list for the base Doc class.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments for the base Doc class.

methods.name: geoparser.geodoc.geodoc.GeoDoc.locations
documentation:
Get location information for all toponyms in the document.

Returns:
    List[Dict[str, Any]]: A list of dictionaries containing location data for toponyms.

methods.name: geoparser.geodoc.geodoc.GeoDoc.toponyms
documentation:
Retrieve the toponyms identified in the document.

Returns:
    Tuple[GeoSpan, ...]: Tuple of GeoSpan objects representing toponyms.
Custom spaCy Doc class extended for geoparsing.

Create a textual description for a location.

Args:

Returns:

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.Gazetteer.get_location_description
documentation:
Get the location description by invoking the abstract method.

Args:

Returns:

Abstract base class for gazetteers.

classes.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the LocalDBGazetteer.

Args:
    gazetteer_name (str): Name of the gazetteer.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.connect
documentation:
Decorator to initiate a database connection before a function call.

Args:
    func (Callable): Function to wrap.

Returns:
    Callable: Wrapped function with database connection.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.commit
documentation:
Decorator to commit changes to the database after a function call.

Args:
    func (Callable): Function to wrap.

Returns:
    Callable: Wrapped function with database commit.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.close
documentation:
Decorator to close the database connection after a function call.

Args:
    func (Callable): Function to wrap.

Returns:
    Callable: Wrapped function with database closure.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._initiate_connection
documentation: Initiate a new database connection if one doesn't exist.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._close_connection
documentation: Close the existing database connection.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._commit
documentation: Commit the current transaction to the database.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._get_cursor
documentation:
Get a cursor from the current database connection.

Returns:
    sqlite3.Cursor: Database cursor object.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.execute_query
documentation:
Execute a SQL query and fetch all results.

Args:
    query (str): SQL query to execute.
    params (Optional[tuple], optional): Parameters for the SQL query.

Returns:
    List[Any]: List of query results.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.setup_database
documentation: Set up the database by downloading and loading data.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.clean_dir
documentation:
Clean the data directory by removing unnecessary files.

Args:
    keep_db (bool, optional): Whether to keep the database files. Defaults to False.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._drop_redundant_tables
documentation: Drop redundant tables from the database and perform vacuuming.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._download_file
documentation:
Download dataset files if they do not exist locally.

Args:
    dataset (GazetteerData): Dataset configuration object.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._extract_zip
documentation:
Extract specific files from a zip archive.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the zip file.
    extracted_files (List[str]): List of files to extract.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._load_data
documentation:
Load data from the dataset into the database.

Args:
    dataset (GazetteerData): Dataset configuration object.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._read_file
documentation:
Read a file and yield data in chunks.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the file.
    columns (List[str], optional): List of columns to read.
    skiprows (Union[int, List[int], Callable], optional): Rows to skip.
    chunksize (int, optional): Number of rows per chunk.

Yields:
    Iterator[pd.DataFrame]: Iterator over DataFrame chunks.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._create_data_table
documentation:
Create a data table in the database for the dataset.

Args:
    dataset (GazetteerData): Dataset configuration object.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._populate_data_table
documentation:
Populate the data table with dataset content.

Args:
    dataset (GazetteerData): Dataset configuration object.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._create_names_table
documentation: Create the 'names' table in the database.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._populate_names_table
documentation:
Populate the 'names' table with location names.

Args:
    chunksize (int, optional): Number of rows per chunk. Defaults to 100000.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._create_names_fts_table
documentation: Create the full-text search virtual table 'names_fts'.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._populate_names_fts_table
documentation: Populate the 'names_fts' full-text search table.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._create_locations_table
documentation: Create the 'locations' table in the database.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._populate_locations_table
documentation: Populate the 'locations' table with location data.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._create_values_table
documentation:
Create tables for distinct values of a filterable attribute.

Args:
    attribute (str): The attribute name.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._populate_values_table
documentation:
Populate tables with distinct values for a filterable attribute.

Args:
    attribute (str): The attribute name.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._validate_filter
documentation:
Validate the filter keys and values.

Args:
    filter (Dict[str, List[str]]): Filter to validate.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the filter contains invalid keys or values.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._get_filter_attributes
documentation:
Get the list of valid filter attributes.

Returns:
    List[str]: List of valid filter attribute names.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._get_filter_values
documentation:
Get the list of valid values for a filter attribute.

Args:
    attribute (str): The attribute name.

Returns:
    List[str]: List of valid values.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer._construct_filter_query
documentation:
Construct additional SQL query text and parameters for filtering.

Args:
    filter (Dict[str, List[str]]): Filter to apply.

Returns:
    Tuple[str, List[str]]: Additional SQL query text and parameters.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.query_candidates
documentation:
Query the database for candidate location IDs matching the toponym.

Args:
    toponym (str): The toponym to search for.
    filter (Optional[Dict[str, List[str]]], optional): Filter to restrict candidate selection.

Returns:
    List[str]: List of candidate location IDs.

methods.name: geoparser.gazetteers.gazetteer.LocalDBGazetteer.query_locations
documentation:
Retrieve location information for a list of location IDs.

Args:
    location_ids (Union[str, List[str]]): List of location IDs.
    batch_size (int, optional): Number of IDs per query batch. Defaults to 500.

Returns:
    List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]: List of dictionaries containing location information.
Gazetteer implementation using a local SQLite database.

classes.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the GeoparserTrainer with inherited Geoparser parameters.

Args:
    *args: Variable length argument list.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer._find_toponym
documentation:
Adjust character indices for imprecise toponym annotations.

Args:
    toponym (str): The toponym text to find.
    doc (GeoDoc): The document to search within.
    start_char (int): The starting character index.
    end_char (int): The ending character index.

Returns:
    Tuple[int, int]: A tuple containing the best match start and end character indices.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer._retokenize_toponym
documentation:
Retokenize the document to ensure the toponym span aligns with spaCy tokens.

Args:
    doc (GeoDoc): The document containing the toponym.
    start_char (int): The starting character index of the toponym.
    end_char (int): The ending character index of the toponym.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer._load_json_file
documentation:
Load a JSON annotation file and transform it into a list of dictionaries.

Args:
    json_file_path (Union[str, Path]): Path to the JSON annotation file.

Returns:
    List[Dict[str, Any]]: A list of dictionaries, where each dictionary represents a document with toponym annotations.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer.annotate
documentation:
Load annotations with toponym spans and gold location IDs.

Args:
    corpus (Union[List[Dict[str, Any]], str, Path]): Either a list of dictionaries containing text and toponym annotations or a path to a JSON annotation file.
    include_unmatched (bool, optional): Whether to include spaCy-unmatched annotations. Defaults to False.

Returns:
    List[GeoDoc]: A list of annotated GeoDoc objects.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer._calculate_auc
documentation:
Calculate the Area Under the Curve (AUC) for error distances.

Args:
    distances (List[float]): List of error distances between predicted and gold locations.

Returns:
    float: The calculated AUC value.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate the model on a list of annotated documents.

Args:
    eval_docs (List[GeoDoc]): List of annotated GeoDoc objects for evaluation.

Returns:
    Dict[str, float]: A dictionary containing evaluation metrics.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer._prepare_training_data
documentation:
Prepare the training data from annotated documents.

Args:
    docs (List[GeoDoc]): List of annotated GeoDoc objects for training.

Returns:
    Dataset: A HuggingFace Dataset containing training examples.

methods.name: geoparser.trainer.trainer.GeoparserTrainer.train
documentation:
Train the toponym disambiguation model using the prepared training data.

Args:
    train_docs (List[GeoDoc]): List of annotated GeoDoc objects for training.
    output_path (str): Directory path to save the trained model.
    epochs (int, optional): Number of training epochs. Defaults to 1.
    batch_size (int, optional): Training batch size. Defaults to 8.
Class for training the Geoparser's toponym disambiguation model.

classes.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Geoparser with specified spaCy model, transformer model, and gazetteer.

Args:
    spacy_model (str): Name of the spaCy model to use for NER.
    transformer_model (str): Name or path of the SentenceTransformer model.
    gazetteer (str): Name of the gazetteer to use.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser.setup_gazetteer
documentation:
Set up the gazetteer.

Args:
    gazetteer (str): Name of the gazetteer to initialize.

Returns:
    Gazetteer: An instance of the specified Gazetteer.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the gazetteer name is invalid.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser.setup_spacy
documentation:
Set up the spaCy NLP pipeline.

Args:
    spacy_model (str): Name of the spaCy model to load.

Returns:
    spacy.language.Language: The loaded spaCy NLP pipeline.

Raises:
    OSError: If the spaCy model is not found.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser.setup_transformer
documentation:
Set up the SentenceTransformer model.

Args:
    transformer_model (str): Name or path of the transformer model to load.

Returns:
    SentenceTransformer: The loaded SentenceTransformer model.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser.parse
documentation:
Perform full geoparsing (recognition and resolution) on a list of texts.

Args:
    texts (List[str]): List of input texts to geoparse.
    batch_size (int): Batch size for processing texts.
    filter (Optional[Dict[str, List[str]]], optional): Filter to restrict candidate selection.

Returns:
    List[GeoDoc]: List of GeoDoc objects containing geoparsed information.

Raises:
    TypeError: If input is not a list of strings.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser.recognize
documentation:
Perform toponym recognition on a list of texts.

Args:
    texts (List[str]): List of input texts.
    batch_size (int): Batch size for processing texts.

Returns:
    List[GeoDoc]: List of GeoDoc objects with recognized toponyms.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser.resolve
documentation:
Perform toponym resolution on a list of GeoDocs.

Args:
    docs (List[GeoDoc]): List of GeoDoc objects with recognized toponyms.
    batch_size (int): Batch size for processing.
    filter (Optional[Dict[str, List[str]]], optional): Filter to restrict candidate selection.

Returns:
    List[GeoDoc]: List of GeoDoc objects with resolved toponyms.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser._get_candidate_ids
documentation:
Collect all candidate location IDs from the toponyms in a list of GeoDocs.

Args:
    docs (List[GeoDoc]): List of GeoDoc objects.
    filter (Optional[Dict[str, List[str]]], optional): Filter to restrict candidate selection.

Returns:
    List[str]: List of unique candidate location IDs.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser._get_candidate_embeddings_lookup
documentation:
Generate embeddings for candidate locations.

Args:
    candidate_ids (List[str]): List of candidate location IDs.
    batch_size (int): Batch size for encoding.

Returns:
    Dict[str, torch.Tensor]: Dictionary mapping candidate IDs to embeddings.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser._get_toponym_embeddings
documentation:
Generate embeddings for all toponyms in a list of GeoDocs.

Args:
    docs (List[GeoDoc]): List of GeoDoc objects.
    batch_size (int): Batch size for encoding.

Returns:
    torch.Tensor: Tensor containing embeddings for toponym contexts.

methods.name: geoparser.geoparser.geoparser.Geoparser._resolve_toponym
documentation:
Resolve a single toponym by comparing embeddings.

Args:
    candidate_embeddings_lookup (Dict[str, torch.Tensor]): Lookup of candidate embeddings.
    candidate_ids (List[str]): List of candidate IDs for the toponym.
    toponym_embeddings (torch.Tensor): Embeddings of toponym contexts.
    toponym_index (int): Index of the current toponym in embeddings.

Returns:
    Tuple[str, float]: The predicted candidate ID and its similarity score.
Main class for performing geoparsing operations.
